content.text:
# MLB Career Comparison: Derek Jeter vs. Albert Pujols

| Category | Derek Jeter | Albert Pujols |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| Primary Position | Shortstop | First Baseman / Designated Hitter |
| Bats / Throws | Right / Right | Right / Right |
| MLB Debut | 1995 | 2001 |
| Final Season | 2014 | 2022 |
| Career Length | 20 seasons | 22 seasons |
| Teams | New York Yankees (1995–2014) | St. Louis Cardinals (2001–2011, 2022), Los Angeles Angels (2012–2021), Los Angeles Dodgers (2021) |
| Career Hits | 3,465 | 3,384 |
| Home Runs | 260 | 703 |
| Runs Batted In (RBI) | 1,311 | 2,218 |
| Batting Average | .310 | .296 |
| All-Star Selections | 14 | 11 |
| MVP Awards | 0 | 3 |
| World Series Titles | 5 (1996, 1998, 1999, 2000, 2009) | 2 (2006, 2011) |
| Gold Glove Awards | 5 | 2 |
| Silver Slugger Awards | 5 | 6 |
| Rookie of the Year | 1996 | 2001 |
| Hall of Fame | Inducted 2020 | Eligible (first ballot in 2028) |

**Quick Summary:**
- **Derek Jeter** was a defensive and clutch-hitting icon at shortstop, known for his consistency, leadership, and championship pedigree with the Yankees.
- **Albert Pujols** was one of the greatest power hitters in history, ranking among the all-time leaders in home runs and RBIs, with elite offensive dominance especially in his Cardinals years.

Let me know if you'd like a deeper statistical breakdown or postseason comparison!
